context_prose: E15 c4 skill is the markdown skill body Claude Code loads when the user invokes `/c4 <sub-action>`. Unlike its sibling skills, it does NOT shell out to the engram CLI binary — its body instructs the agent to use `targ c4-*` build/audit/registry/render targets and to edit files under `architecture/c4/` directly. The properties below are behavioral guarantees the skill text imposes on the agent: dispatch rules, conflict-resolution discipline, propagation discipline, ID/format invariants, and untested-property handling. Tested rows point at the pressure-test markdown captures under `skills/c4/tests/`; rules with no pressure-test capture are marked **⚠ UNTESTED**.

edges.label: loads skill body on /command or auto-trigger

legend_items: **External** — grey (Claude Code, the harness that loads the skill body).
**R-edges** — solid; load relationships from the parent c3.
name: c4-skill

properties.name: Sub-action dispatch
properties.notes: Dispatch table is content-level guidance; no test exercises every sub-action.
properties.statement: For all invocations of `/c4 <sub-action> [args]`, the skill instructs the agent to dispatch on intent to exactly one of {create, update, review, audit} and follow the workflow for that sub-action.

properties.name: Ask-don't-guess on conflict
properties.notes: Pressure test 1: subagent surfaced 4 distinct conflicts and held the draft pending answers.
properties.statement: For all draft sessions where code reality and intent (docs / commit bodies / session memory) disagree, the skill instructs the agent to STOP drafting, present BOTH views, and ask the user which is correct — never silently pick one.

properties.name: Distinct conflicts asked separately
properties.notes: Implicit in rule 1; pressure test 1 verifies subagent surfaced 4 distinct, separable conflicts.
properties.statement: For all sets of N independent code/intent conflicts found while drafting, the skill instructs the agent to surface each conflict as a separate question — not to batch them into a single combined ask.

properties.name: Never invent test pointers
properties.notes: Pressure test 3: subagent marked 1 of 6 properties UNTESTED rather than fabricating a pointer.
properties.statement: For all L4 properties whose test does not exist, the skill instructs the agent to mark the row **⚠ UNTESTED** verbatim — never fabricate a test link and never omit the property to make the ledger cleaner.

properties.name: Per-file approval for non-target edits
properties.notes: Pressure test 2: subagent presented L1-parent and L3-child edits as separate apply/skip/defer proposals, not silent edits.
properties.statement: For all proposed edits to files other than the explicit target of the current sub-action, the skill instructs the agent to present each as a separate proposal with `[a]pply` / `[s]kip` / `[d]efer` — never silently edit a non-target file.

properties.name: Mermaid classDef convention
properties.notes: Baseline GREEN test verifies the classDef block is present in the produced L1 draft.
properties.statement: For all L1–L3 diagrams the skill produces, the mermaid block opens with the project's classDef block (`person`, `external`, `container`, `component`) and every node is assigned its class via `:::` syntax or trailing `class` directives.

properties.name: Cross-link in body, no index
properties.notes: Baseline GREEN test verifies explicit Cross-links section naming the expected L2 file.
properties.statement: For all C4 files the skill produces or updates, parent and children are named directly in the file body (relative paths) — there is no separate index file mediating cross-links.

properties.name: Every element and edge carries an ID
properties.notes: L4 ledgers use `P<n>` for properties (separate namespace).
properties.statement: For all L1–L3 diagrams, every catalog row is tagged `E<n>`, every relationships row `R<n>`, and the same IDs appear inside the mermaid node and edge labels; mismatches between diagram IDs and catalog/relationship IDs are reported as drift findings by `review` and `audit`.

properties.name: DI back-edges use D-namespace and dotted style
properties.notes: Per-dep decomposition lives in the consumer's L4 Dependency Manifest; reciprocal entries in the wirer's L4 DI Wires.
properties.statement: For all DI dependencies wired by another component into the focus, the skill instructs the agent to draw a dotted edge `consumer → wirer` labelled `D<n>` on both the parent L3 diagram and the L4 context strip; D-ids are independent of R-ids and one D-id per (consumer, wirer) pair.

properties.name: All diagrams pre-rendered to SVG
properties.notes: GitHub's mermaid renderer lacks the ELK layout engine the project relies on for clean bidirectional R/D edges.
properties.statement: For all C4 diagrams produced or updated by the skill, the markdown embeds an SVG via `![alt](svg/...)` rather than an inline ` ```mermaid ` block; both `.mmd` source and `.svg` output live under `architecture/c4/svg/` and are committed.

properties.name: L1/L3 use JSON-spec → build → audit
properties.notes: Mechanical work is offloaded to targ targets so only judgment remains in the LLM.
properties.statement: For all `create` / `update` actions at L1 and L3, the skill instructs the agent to author or edit `architecture/c4/c<n>-<name>.json`, run `targ c4-l<n>-build --input ... --noconfirm`, and verify zero findings via `targ c4-audit --file ...` — never hand-write the markdown.

properties.name: Approval before write
properties.notes: Pressure test 1: subagent held the draft pending user answers, did not write the file.
properties.statement: For all `create` invocations, the skill instructs the agent to show the full draft to the user and get explicit approval before writing the file at `architecture/c4/c<level>-<name>.md`.

properties.name: Refines parent element
properties.notes: L1 has no parent — explicitly skipped in the L1 sub-workflow.
properties.statement: For all `create <level> <name>` with `level > 1`, the skill instructs the agent to first read the parent-level file and ensure the new diagram refines an existing element of its parent.

properties.name: Propagation sweep is required
properties.notes: Pressure test 2: subagent proposed all three required diffs (target L2, parent L1, child L3).
properties.statement: For all `create` or `update` actions, the skill instructs the agent to perform the four-step Propagation Discipline sweep: update the parent's `cross_links.refined_by`, rebuild siblings, walk children, and run `targ c4-audit` + `targ c4-registry` — skipping creates registry findings on the next audit.

properties.name: Idempotent rebuilds are propagation, not edits
properties.notes: Reconciles propagation against the non-target-edit rule (P5).
properties.statement: For all sibling and child rebuilds whose only diff is in auto-generated sections (mermaid, catalog, cross-links emitted by `c4-l*-build`), the skill classifies the rebuild as propagation that does NOT require per-file approval; JSON edits to non-target files DO require it.

properties.name: Drift Notes persist deferred work
properties.notes: Pressure test 3: subagent recorded a drift note flagging the UNTESTED coverage gap.
properties.statement: For all propagation proposals the user defers and all code/intent gaps the user chooses not to resolve, the skill instructs the agent to append a dated entry to a `## Drift Notes` section at the bottom of the target file — drift notes never silently disappear.

properties.name: Review and audit are read-only
properties.notes: Read-only contract distinguishes review/audit from create/update.
properties.statement: For all `review <name>` and `audit` invocations, the skill instructs the agent to produce a drift / mismatch report and make NO edits to any file.

properties.name: L4 row format
properties.notes: Pressure test 3 verifies universally-quantified statements ("For all input lines L, …").
properties.statement: For all rows of an L4 ledger the skill produces, each row carries the columns Property, Statement, Enforced at (`file:line` link), Tested at (`test_file:line` link or **⚠ UNTESTED**), and Notes — and the Statement begins with a universal quantifier ("For all …").

properties.name: One property per row
properties.notes: Anti-pattern guard from property-ledger-format.md.
properties.statement: For all L4 ledger rows, each row carries exactly one property — the skill forbids smuggling two properties into one Statement via `and`.

properties.name: Statement is guarantee, not implementation
properties.notes: Anti-pattern table maps vague claims to concrete guarantees.
properties.statement: For all L4 properties, the Statement column states the contract for ALL inputs (the durable guarantee), not the mechanism — implementation details belong in Notes.

properties.name: DI-via-mocks counts as tested
properties.notes: Aligned with the project DI principle: test at the seam the property is about.
properties.statement: For all L4 properties asserted by a unit test that injects mocks for the focus component's deps, the skill classifies the property as TESTED — never downgrades to UNTESTED merely because no integration test exists; integration coverage, if desired, is captured as a separate property.

properties.name: Update classifies the change
properties.notes: Pressure test 2 exercises the rename-classification path.
properties.statement: For all `update <name>` invocations, the skill instructs the agent to classify the change as one of {renamed element, removed element, new element, changed responsibility/relationship, L3 code-pointer change} and propagate accordingly to parents, siblings, and children.

properties.name: Diagrams live under architecture/c4
properties.notes: Path convention is structural; the audit and registry both rely on it.
properties.statement: For all C4 files the skill produces, the file path is `architecture/c4/c<level>-<name>.md` — the skill never writes diagrams elsewhere in the repo.

properties.name: Code-grounded drafts
properties.notes: Baseline GREEN: subagent ran 10 tool uses against repo source, vs RED baseline that ran zero.
properties.statement: For all `create` and `update` invocations at L3/L4, the skill instructs the agent to deep-read the specific packages/files in scope plus intent sources (CLAUDE.md, docs/, scoped `git log --format=full`, recent session memory via `engram recall`) before drafting.
